programmes.show_description: There is talk of a wildcat strike among drivers - if any Hatley staff join it, the station could be closed. S2 E5: Lucky Strike
With the time portal still haywire, Ron makes it back to the forties, where he is out of place and makes Gary's life even more difficult. S5 E4: Mairzy Doats
More mayhem with hapless bumbler Mr. Bean as he struggles to wait in line for an appointment and thinks up some unusual methods to help him sleep. E13: Goodnight Mr. Bean
What looks like a simple drugs drop at a building site turns into a murder case, and Alex is haunted by thoughts of her parents' death. S2 E7
Sandra and Derek spend Christmas apart; as the women spend Christmas together, Carol's dad heads to a pub in Wales with the men. S8 E8: Open Your Eyes and It Still Hasn't Gone
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Brenda gets soaking wet while she and Malcolm are returning from bird watching; as Madge has to wash all Brenda's clothes, everyone else is talking about redundancy or job changes. S2 E1: Falling
Sharon's disapproval of Tracey dating policeman Barry and apparently overlooking Garth's feelings for his dad leads to a major quarrel between the sisters. S12 E3: Tombstone Blues
Mr. Bean buys a TV and a new camera but has issues with both; meanwhile, he ends up in another pickle when he goes to a magic show and a disco. E4: Mr. Bean Goes to Town
Returning from a boat trip to Tangier, Amy is approached by a Spaniard who asks her to deliver a box allegedly containing the ashes of his dead friend Pepe to his widowed mother. S2 E5: Pepe

Joey claims an 'incontinence allowance' for Grandad and Billy enters the unscrupulous world of antiques. S1 E1
Sandra is preparing for marriage and trying to get into shape, and Lucian is very upset that someone has stolen all his rabbits. S8 E6: The Edge
Ethel has fallen in love with an American pilot called Joe, but Wilfred is not at all pleased. S2 E6: Love is a Very Splendid Thing
Ivy finds out the truth about Alf's orphans, and Miss Cissy decides to go into politics to Lord Meldrum's disgust. S3 E3 Mrs Lipton's Nasty Turn
Jo is going on a date with Philip but has forgotten that she is meant to be babysitting; Chrissy agrees to take her place and persuades Robin to come with her. S2 E5: Two Foot Two, Eyes Of Blue
Danielle tells a pestering teacher that she is engaged to Mr. Brown. S1 E11: A Point of Honour
Alf and Bert join the golf club. After a poor round, they decide to improve their standing by faking their scores. S7 E5: A Whole in One

When Arthur's GP orders him to cut down on booze and cigars, his grip on reality is called into question. S8 E6: The Last Temptation of Daley
Ethel has fallen in love with an American pilot called Joe, but Wilfred is not at all pleased. S2 E6: Love is a Very Splendid Thing
With a financial crisis looming, Gary has to do what he promised he would never do, interfere with history. But history bites back. S5 E5: Pennies from Heaven
The blunderingly inept master of disaster goes for a haircut - but ends up styling the other customers' hair - and causes chaos at a school fun day. E14: Hair by Mr. Bean of London
Alex has caught a hospital infection and the doctors in 2008 are losing hope for her - a wedge develops between her and Gene and it threatens to tear the team apart. S2 E8
Sandra is home alone while Derek is at work, so they agree to let the spare room as company for Sandra. Who is Dawn and what is her link to Carol? S9 E1: There's No Place Like Away from Home
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Pamela gets even more depressed at work as Terry gets roped into an effort to get her back out into circulation, and Brenda isn't happy when she spots Malcolm talking to another woman. S2 E2: Dancing
When Tracey has enough of Dorien entertaining gentlemen callers and Sharon not bringing in a living wage, she throws them both out and they end up in Sharon's old bedsit. S12 E4: Going, Going, Gone
Mr. Bean gets to an appointment with the dentist after oversleeping and has trouble in the park with a bothersome fly. E5: The Trouble with Mr. Bean
David is less than happy to find that the obnoxious Kev Wilson, a man who has always done better than him and let him know it since childhood, is staying at the hotel. S2 E6: Snap

Billy learns that opals are bad luck when he buys a ring for Julie. S1 E2
The day of the wedding has arrived for Sandra and Derek, but will Sandra marry him or let him go when Derek announces he has been offered a role in Africa? S8 E7: The Struggle
The annual bowls club outing seems to provide an ideal opportunity for Cecil to be alone with May. S2 E7: A Bowl In The Hand
Romance is in the air for Poppy and Lady Lavender's parrot, and Lord Meldrum decides to learn about the lower classes and so invites the factory workers to dinner. S3 E4 Meet the Workers
Robin has failed his cookery exam and considers moving back to Southampton, but the girls are far from pleased at the thought of his mate Larry moving in. S2 E6: Carry Me Back to Old Southampton

Mr. Brown tells the class the story of how he was left in an orphanage when he was a baby. However, his mother and father might be a lot closer to him than he ever imagined. S1 E12: How's Your Father?
Alf loses his job and comes home to find he may have been burgled. It's an unhappy birthday but a surprise is in store. S7 E6: Unemployment

Billy learns that opals are bad luck when he buys a ring for Julie. S1 E2
Ray is given the task of minding a racing pigeon that is due to race from London to Bradford. S8 E7: A Bird in the Hand is Worth Two in Shepherd's Bush
